version_notes: v1.1.5 (2026-05-01, conceptual coverage expansion): Tier A grows from 17 → 33 phrases to address the President's feedback that the title list is 'a little narrow' relative to what Enterprise Talent Solutions sees in the wild. The 17-phrase v1.1.4 list resolved to ~10 conceptual buckets after collapsing variants; this pass adds five new buckets that were entirely missing from the API filter (so the API never even returned candidates for them): (1) Operations technician/engineer family — `critical operations technician/engineer`, `mission critical operations technician/engineer`, `data center critical operations technician`. Standard titles at Equinix, Digital Realty, hyperscalers. (2) Operator suffix family — `data center operator`, `mission critical operator`, `critical environment operator`, `critical facilities operator`. Common at hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta) which use 'operator' instead of 'technician'. (3) Power-side discipline — `ups technician`, `generator technician`, `critical power technician`. (4) Cooling-side discipline — `critical cooling technician`, `data center cooling technician`. (5) DCIM/site family — `dcim technician`, `data center site engineer`. Industry_context expanded with `ups`, `uninterruptible power`, `n+1 redundancy`, `2n redundancy`, `concurrent maintainability`, `bms`, `building management system`, `dcim`, `pue`, `chiller plant`, `crac unit`, `crah`, `chilled water` — these DC-specific phrases let the gate credit JDs that describe a real data center using its industry vocabulary. Tier B left unchanged at 13 phrases (ETS review pending on HVAC/cooling/operator generic suffixes; those have higher false-positive risk and should land in v1.1.6 only after the curriculum-strength signal is confirmed). v1.1.4 (2026-04-28, post-share precision pass): added `description_disqualifiers.wrong_discipline_it` — hard-rejects Tier A title matches whose description is unambiguously IT-side data-center work (rack-and-stack, fiber/copper cabling, RMAs, GPU clusters, hardware troubleshooting, OS administration) AND lacks ANY electromechanical/safety vocabulary. Catches the 'IT data center technician' archetype: title says CFT, work is the IT track. Direct evidence: TEKsystems Phoenix DC Tech (2026-04-28, scored 43 LOW) and Nebius Kansas City Data Center Technician Early Talent (2026-04-28, scored 46 LOW) both flagged by Imran as wrong-discipline rather than 'adjacent' — different Per Scholas program (IT Support / Networking) entirely. Rule fires when ≥2 IT-side indicators appear AND zero CFT-side signals (UPS, generator, switchgear, ATS, PDU, HVAC, EPA 608, NFPA 70E, OSHA 10, chiller, BMS, BAS, CRAC, CRAH, MOP, EOP, LOTO, preventative maintenance) appear in the same description. The CFT-side suppressor protects legitimate hybrid roles where a real CFT job mentions some IT-adjacent work in passing. Rejection reason set to 'Wrong discipline: IT data center role (better fit for IT Support / Networking program)' so the breakdown panel shows it as its own bucket. v1.1.3 (2026-04-27, post-first-fetch §C precision pass): added Tier B `score_if_no_industry_context: 0` — when a Tier B title matches but ZERO industry-context phrases ('mission critical', 'data center', 'hyperscale', 'colocation', 'uptime', '24/7', etc.) appear in the description, the title score collapses to 0 regardless of core-skill count. Direct evidence: the Cushman & Wakefield 'Maintenance Technician' Atlanta posting from the first fetch (2026-04-27) scored 68 MEDIUM via Tier B 25 + 4 generic building-trade skills (HVAC/EPA 608/EOP/Preventative Maintenance) + watchlist hit (facilities_services category) + no DC industry context — confirmed by Imran via source-URL spot-check as a non-fit (apartment/office maintenance). Under v1.1.3 this same job lands in LOW. Stage 2 (`score_if_unverified` for low core-skill count) is preserved and unchanged. v1.1.2 (2026-04-27, post-first-fetch §C tuning, additive): closed the singular/plural gap on 'facility' titles. Tier A previously had 'critical facilities technician', 'data center facilities technician', 'mission critical facilities technician' (plural only). Added singular forms 'critical facility technician', 'data center facility technician', 'mission critical facility technician' so postings using the singular noun (the more common real-world form per the EDC Data Center Facility Technician posting observed on ZipRecruiter) match correctly. v1.1.1 (2026-04-27, post-first-fetch §C tuning, additive): added 'engineering operations technician' to Tier A — Amazon's preferred title for Data Center technicians at AWS DC sites (DCC Communities). Verified against RapidAPI active-ats-7d: 10+ active postings nationwide (Manassas VA, Boardman OR, Gilroy CA, San Francisco) under organization='Amazon', source=amazon.jobs. Atlanta instance was outside the 7-day window during first fetch but expected to recur. Substring matching also covers 'Night Shift Engineering Operations Technician'; seniority exclusions (sr./senior/lead/iii) still bite first. v1.1.0 (2026-04-27, pre-fetch addendum pass): §A1 word-boundary skill matching (optional `match_mode` on SkillEntry; auto-classified for ≤4-char or all-caps forms; trailing 's' allowed for plurals). §A2 regex-based experience disqualifier with proximity-to-required-word gate; `description_disqualifiers.experience` now an object {max_years_allowed: 3, patterns: [...]}. §A3 + §B3 rule-based tags (top-level `tags` block) evaluated independently of titleTier; legacy HEALTHCARE_CONTEXT tag retired; tier C/D `tag` field kept as fallback. §A5 Tier A title list extended with hyperscaler 'engineer' titles (Microsoft/Google/Meta/AWS). §B4 employer_watchlist.categories restructured to {is_healthcare, employers} so §A4 can use a healthcare-employer match as a Tier D gate signal. KNOWN ISSUE (§A6, queued for post-fetch tuning): OSHA 10, NFPA 70E, EPA 608 appear in BOTH core_skills and certifications and currently double-count (8 + 5 = 13 pts per single mention). Per Imran 2026-04-27 this is unintended; will be deduplicated in §C. v1.0.0 was the initial taxonomy derived from UCI 2137 (15 weeks, 412.5 instructional hours).

A.description: Direct synonyms — auto-pass to skill verification, highest title score (40 pts). §A5: hyperscaler 'engineer' titles (Microsoft/Google/Meta/AWS) added 2026-04-27 — these employers use 'Data Center Engineer' for what others call 'Data Center Technician'. Level-suffixed variants ('engineer iii', 'engineer ii') are still rejected via title_exclusions.seniority, which runs before tier matching. v1.1.1 (post-first-fetch §C tuning): added 'engineering operations technician' — Amazon's canonical DC tech title at AWS sites; substring-matches 'Night Shift Engineering Operations Technician' too. v1.1.2: added singular 'facility' variants alongside the plural 'facilities' forms — substring matching is strict, and real-world postings use both.

B.description: Family match — two-stage demotion. (1) v1.1.3: requires at least 1 industry-context phrase (data center / mission critical / hyperscale / etc.) to retain ANY title score; otherwise collapses to 0 to suppress false positives like the Cushman maintenance pattern. (2) v1.1.0: even with industry context, requires ≥ 4 core skills to keep the full 25; else drops to 10 (score_if_unverified).

C.description: Building Automation Track — graduates have partial qualification (PLC + BACnet/Modbus + cybersecurity from outline). Tagged BAS_TRACK.

D.description: Healthcare track — same skills, hospital-specific titles. Requires healthcare context to qualify. Tagged HEALTHCARE_TRACK.

title_exclusions.description: Hard reject if title contains any of these substrings.

description_disqualifiers.description: Hard reject if description_text contains any of these phrases.

experience.description: §A2: regex-based experience filter. Reject when a captured year-count > max_years_allowed AND a 'required'-class word appears within ±50 chars of the match. Patterns capture both digit and spelled-out forms; the spelled standalone pattern relies on the proximity gate to suppress incidental phrases (e.g. 'four years ago').

wrong_discipline_it.description: v1.1.4: hard-reject IT-side data-center technician roles that match a CFT title but describe IT/networking work (rack-and-stack, hardware deployment, RMAs, OS admin) instead of electromechanical/facilities work. Fires when ≥`min_indicators` IT-side phrases appear in the description AND zero CFT-side `absent_signals` appear. The absent_signals check is critical — a real CFT job that happens to mention 'patch panels' once still has UPS/HVAC/MOP/EOP/etc. somewhere in the description and is preserved. Better fit for the IT Support / Networking Per Scholas program.

core_skills.description: Curriculum-confirmed skills taught extensively (≥4 days each in UCI 2137). Each match = 8 pts, capped at 40.

specialized_skills.description: Hands-on lab skills with named systems. Each match = 4 pts, capped at 20.

bonus_skills.description: Supporting topics. Each match = 1 pt, capped at 5. ONLY counts if at least one core skill OR industry context also matched (prevents manufacturing-floor false positives).

industry_context.description: Mission-critical environment signals. Each match = 3 pts, capped at 10.

healthcare_context.description: Phrases indicating healthcare facility (qualifies Tier D titles).

certifications.description: Per Scholas-earned certifications. Each match = 5 pts, capped at 15.

employer_watchlist.description: Known target employers. Match = +5 pts. §B4 (v1.1.0): each category now carries `is_healthcare` metadata so the Tier D gate (§A4) can use a healthcare-employer match as a healthcare context signal, in addition to description phrases and `linkedin_org_industry`.

post_fetch_penalty.description: If ai_experience_level is 2-5 BUT description requires >3 years, deduct 10 points.

geography.description: Per-campus geofencing using haversine distance from campus coords against jobs' lats_derived/lngs_derived.

formula: + bonus_skills_score (count * 1, capped at 5; requires core_match >= 1 OR industry_match >= 1)
Hard rejects (return REJECT regardless of score):

BAS_TRACK.description: Strong building-automation signal regardless of title tier (e.g., a Tier A CFT role heavy in BACnet/Modbus/PLC content).

HEALTHCARE_TRACK.description: Healthcare facility employer, or strong healthcare context in title/description/industry.
